description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: bacterial infectious disorder contracted by consumption of food or drink contaminated with Salmonella typhi. This disorder is common in developing countries and can be treated with antibiotics.
description_wiki: Typhoid fever, also known as typhoid, is an orally contracted disease caused by Salmonella enterica serotype Typhi bacteria, also called Salmonella Typhi.
Baptists are a Protestant tradition of Christianity distinguished by baptizing only believers (believer's baptism) and doing so by total immersion.
Franklin County is a county in the Northeast region of the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: human settlement in Oglethorpe County, Georgia, United States of America
description_wiki: Stephens is an unincorporated community in Oglethorpe County, Georgia, United States.

description: county seat in Franklin County, Georgia, USA
description_wiki: Carnesville is a city in Franklin County, Georgia, United States, and the county seat.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Tennessee ( , locally ), officially the State of Tennessee, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: consolidated city-county and county seat of Clarke County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Athens is a consolidated city-county in the U.S. state of Georgia.
